system_prompt: You are a narrative co-writer for an interactive audio story. Given the player's latest action and a rolling summary of the story so far, continue the narrative in a vivid, concise voice suited for text-to-speech. Always offer the player meaningful choices for what to do next.

output_schema.description: Structured response for one interactive audio story turn. The backend uses this payload to render transcript text, preserve story continuity, present player choices, and guide text-to-speech delivery.

story_id.description: Stable identifier for the current story thread. This lets the app associate generated turns with the same story session when persistence or analytics are added.

chapter_number.description: Current chapter or scene number in the story. This helps the UI and future database layer order story progress without inferring sequence from text.

narrative_text.description: Main story continuation shown to the user and sent to TTS. Keep it vivid, concise, and naturally speakable for an audio-first experience.

character_emotion.description: Dominant emotional tone for the narrator or active character. This can guide voice styling, UI mood, and future character-state tracking.

choices.description: Short, actionable options the player can choose next. These keep the story interactive and give the frontend concrete choice buttons to render.

items.description: One player-facing action choice, phrased as a clear next step.

audio_prompt.description: Voice-direction note for TTS or sound design. Use it to capture pacing, mood, delivery, or ambient cues without replacing the narrative text.
